---
title: "CDADE Results Report"
author: "CDADE Team"
date: "2026-07-05"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
---

# Results

## Experiment summary

The current run completed the full DVC workflow and generated evaluation outputs from the injected SIVEP-based experiment. The results are stored in results/metrics.json and the per-method summaries under results/evaluation.

## Metrics summary

| Method | AUC-PR | NAB | Precision | Recall | F1 |
| --- | ---: | ---: | ---: | ---: | ---: |
| CDADE | 1.000 | 1.000 | 1.000 | 1.000 | 1.000 |
| B1 Farrington | 1.000 | 0.000 | 1.000 | 0.500 | 0.667 |
| B2 Best single | 1.000 | 1.000 | 1.000 | 0.538 | 0.700 |
| B3 Ensemble average | 1.000 | 0.000 | 1.000 | 0.500 | 0.667 |
| B4 Static top-k | 1.000 | 0.000 | 1.000 | 0.500 | 0.667 |
| B5 Reconciliation + EVT | 1.000 | 1.000 | 1.000 | 1.000 | 1.000 |

## Statistical comparison

The statistical stage was executed, but the protocol stopped early because the current experiment only includes one dataset. The Friedman test therefore reported that a multi-dataset comparison was not meaningful, and the downstream Wilcoxon, Diebold-Mariano, and Cliff's delta outputs were written as placeholders with non-informative values. This is consistent with the README's intended protocol, which requires multiple datasets to make the hypothesis-testing procedure meaningful.

## Interpretation

On this first end-to-end run, CDADE and B5 matched the strongest observed performance on the injected dataset. The results support the implementation workflow and the report structure described in the README, but they should be interpreted as a reproducibility smoke test rather than a final multi-dataset claim.
